artifacts.authority_safety_posture: No authority mutation, replay, or continuation may bypass manifest lineage truth.
notes: Immutable after seal except append-only post-seal outcome fields.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can reconstruct why a run was new, reused, replayed, or continued from one manifest spine.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Root artifact for same-manifest reuse, replay child allocation, and recovery child lineage.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: RunManifest is the canonical execution, lineage, and sealing truth for request-time control.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A materially significant run allocates or reuses a manifest envelope.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Counterfactual analysis may vary config only under analysis posture and explicit declaration.
notes: Compliance-grade replay forbids non-deterministic allowlists for exact reuse.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Change reviews can point to one frozen config bundle instead of ambient runtime state.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Exact same-attempt recovery and exact replay require identical config freeze semantics.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ConfigFreeze captures the lawful config basis for live, replay, nightly, and release flows.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Freeze-before-decision and freeze-before-replay rules resolve stable config.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Fresh live recollection is forbidden while still claiming exact replay.
notes: Retention-limited placeholders must surface explicitly instead of collapsing to not applicable.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can distinguish lawful retained placeholders from missing or corrupted input basis.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Exact replay and same-attempt recovery require readable input freeze plus authoritative intake basis.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: InputFreeze captures the exact input basis later used by replay, recovery, and audits.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Intake and source collection complete before compute, submission, or replay.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Authority recovery and replay attestations read persisted hashes instead of inferring basis from side effects.
notes: Decision and outcome hashes are append-only once published for a manifest version.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Investigations can explain exactly which hash changed between reuse, replay, continuation, and promotion.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Recovery children preserve `execution_basis_hash`; later lineage nodes must not rewrite it in place.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: HashSet is the canonical replay, idempotency, and audit integrity spine.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Manifest sealing derives the execution, manifest, decision, and outcome hashes.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Continuation metadata cannot impersonate transport resume tokens or read-side stability tokens.
notes: Continuation legality is part of branch proof, not a late-stage convenience flag.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operator tooling can explain why a child reuses history without collapsing it into ordinary continuation.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Same-attempt recovery is exact; historically explicit continuation is limited and must stay explicitly marked.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ContinuationSet distinguishes exact recovery, replay, historically explicit continuation, and new-request children.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A child manifest or replay chooses inherited versus freshly resolved basis.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Access binding drift blocks silent replay or resend reuse.
notes: Executable identity includes access decision class, executable scope, masking, approvals, and authentication level.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Access and authority investigations can separate token drift from request-body drift.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Authority recovery may reuse request lineage only when binding lineage remains unchanged.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: FrozenExecutionBinding extends request identity with executable scope and approval context.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Access evaluation resolves the executable request boundary before sealing or authority mutation.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Missing or mismatched pre-seal tape forces fail-closed replay posture.
notes: The first four gate records remain an immutable prefix even when later gates append after seal.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can see whether a run blocked before start or sealed with a complete pre-seal basis.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Exact replay and same-manifest reuse consume the persisted tape instead of recomputing on ambient state.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: preseal_gate_evaluation captures the authoritative pre-start tape and durability boundary.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A manifest approaches seal and must freeze the canonical pre-seal gate prefix.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: No second live start is legal while an active lease still exists.
notes: Claim and first durable publication commit atomically.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Schedulers and operators can distinguish already-active, reclaim-required, and terminal-reuse outcomes without worker logs.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Nightly reclaim and recovery child allocation must read this control object instead of inferring from missing heartbeats.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: manifest_start_claim is the single-writer start lease and first-publication truth for live execution.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A sealed manifest is claimed for the first live post-seal execution or stale recovery successor flow.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Lineage truth is explicit; nearby timestamps or read mirrors cannot substitute for branch proof.
notes: The current corpus names ManifestLineageTrace in prose but does not provide a dedicated schema.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Explorers and audit tooling can show why one candidate won and others were rejected.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Recovery, replay, continuation, and same-manifest reuse remain explainable from one branch artifact.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ManifestLineageTrace is the request-time branch explainer and candidate rejection ledger.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Branch selection chooses reuse, replay, recovery, continuation, or fresh-child allocation.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Counterfactual analysis remains analysis-only and may not mutate authority-facing state.
notes: No replay child may publish replay-visible truth without a linked durable attestation.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators see exact, expected-difference, limited, corrupt, or unexpected mismatch posture from a signed artifact.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Corrupt, incomplete, or retention-limited basis becomes explicit posture instead of silent substitution.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ReplayAttestation is the durable replay-comparison and operator/auditor explanation artifact.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A replay child reaches a persisted decision outcome and must publish durable comparison truth.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Cross-window continuity requires a new nightly identity even if config and inputs are unchanged.
notes: Same-window duplicates reuse batch identity; later windows remain separate control objects.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can trace a batch back to its trigger class, release-verification basis, and policy snapshot.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Recovery reclaim windows link the predecessor explicitly and forbid silent second-batch allocation.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: NightlyBatchIdentityContract is the scheduler dedupe and batch identity tuple.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly trigger allocation freezes same-window dedupe, cross-window continuity, and recovery lineage.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Nightly automation may not bypass manifest leases, authority ambiguity rules, or resend boundaries.
notes: Per-client entries remain explicit even for reuse, defer, escalation, and skip outcomes.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Morning operations can read one batch artifact instead of reconstructing overnight behavior from queues.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Crash recovery reuses predecessor selection and shard truth or escalates if proof is incomplete.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: NightlyBatchRun is the authoritative overnight selection, shard, and quiescence control object.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A nightly control-plane window has been allocated and frozen for one tenant.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Same-window active attempts may defer or reclaim; they may not silently duplicate live execution.
notes: Only execution-capable rows enter shard plans; reuse, defer, escalation, and skip remain off-shard.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Client-level overnight outcomes are visible even when no manifest is executed.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Selection rows survive reclaim and batch restarts; unrelated clients retain explicit outcomes despite shard failure.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: Nightly selection entry is the per-candidate operating decision row for overnight execution.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly portfolio selection evaluates one candidate client-period tuple exactly once.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Customer-visible consequences remain bound to persisted workflow and audit evidence, not free-text summaries.
notes: Publication state and QA are explicit parts of the digest contract.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators receive a single coverage-date digest instead of reconstructing overnight posture manually.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Digest derivation remains pinned to live nightly compliance posture, not replay or analysis.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: OperatorMorningDigest is the deterministic next-morning handoff artifact for overnight control-plane behavior.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly quiescence succeeds far enough to publish one tenant coverage-date handoff summary.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Authority mutations require lineage and binding revalidation after restore or failover.
notes: Control-plane legal truth may not serialize a weaker tier than TIER_0_CONTROL_PLANE.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: SRE and release tooling can prove the tier, RPO/RTO, and reopen policy used by a checkpoint or release.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Tier mappings and reopen policy remain explicit and fail closed when weaker classes appear.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: RecoveryGovernanceContract is the shared checkpoint and release resilience policy object.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Checkpoint and release artifacts bind one shared recovery governance boundary.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Restore of authority-integrated work must rebuild outstanding reconciliation from durable truth.
notes: `READY_FOR_REOPEN` is lawful only after privacy, audit, queue, and authority checks all pass.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Restore readiness remains blocked by typed missing gates instead of silent reopen optimism.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Verified posture requires restore evidence, privacy reconciliation, queue rebuild, and authority revalidation.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: RecoveryCheckpoint is the authoritative restore evidence and reopen-gating artifact for a protected workload.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Scheduled backup cadence or promotion/DR evidence binds a restore-capable checkpoint.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity blocks reopen rather than being erased or hand-waved away.
notes: Privacy reconciliation is a reopen blocker, not optional cleanup.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can distinguish ready-for-reopen, limited, legal-hold-blocked, and proof-preservation-blocked restore posture.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Restore remains blocked until privacy, limitation, and audit continuity become reopen-safe.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: RestorePrivacyReconciliationContract is the authoritative restore privacy blocker and compensating re-erasure record.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A restore or drill may resurrect restricted data and must reconcile privacy before reopen.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Fresh mutation resend is blocked after budget exhaustion or unresolved ambiguity.
notes: The reconciliation control packet is copied onto unresolved SubmissionRecord and ObligationMirror surfaces.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operations can see whether recovery is read-only, idempotent-only, or blocked pending escalation.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Restore, replay, or reclaim reuses the persisted reconciliation control packet rather than recomputing from retries.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: AuthorityInteractionRecord is the durable exchange and reconciliation truth for no-blind-resend safety.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: An authority-bound submission or reconciliation exchange exists or is awaiting clarification.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Authority sandbox evidence may not drift across enabled provider-profile sets.
notes: Ordered arrays are canonicalized before hashing so the same candidate cannot drift across workers.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Release reviewers can reject mixed-candidate evidence from one candidate hash.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Restore drills and client compatibility evidence must remain bound to the exact candidate tuple they verified.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ReleaseCandidateIdentityContract is the shared candidate tuple for release evidence and deployment records.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A promotion candidate or restore drill binds one exact build, schema, config, provider-profile, and client-window tuple.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Closed reader windows and blocked native windows become explicit fail-forward boundaries.
notes: Compatibility gate rows must bind the same candidate hash and compatibility hash across evidence artifacts.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Release tooling can show why rollback remained legal or became fail-forward-only.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Replay and restore require a compatible reader window or an explicitly blocked posture.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: SchemaBundleCompatibilityGateContract is the mutable schema safety boundary around a fixed release candidate.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Promotion or restore evidence claims schema safety for historical manifests, replay, restore, and native-client compatibility.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Authority sandbox coverage remains part of promotion admissibility when suite_family = AUTHORITY_SANDBOX.
notes: Promotion evidence is machine-assembled from first-class artifacts instead of reconstructed dashboards.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Operators can replay the exact gate order, evidence refs, and decision posture from one root object.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Restore drills used for promotion bind the exact checkpoint and candidate tuple into the promotion record.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: ReleaseVerificationManifest is the machine-assembled promotion-evidence root for one candidate.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: Promotion assembles blocking evidence, admissibility posture, and companion artifacts into one durable release root.

artifacts.authority_safety_posture: Release rollback is distinct from legal authority truth; evidence is never deleted to simulate rollback.
notes: FAILED_FORWARD posture is unlawful without a compensating release and named owner.
artifacts.operator_visible_effect: Release operators can see whether a rollout remained rollback-safe or crossed into fail-forward-only posture.
artifacts.recovery_posture: Closed schema windows force FAIL_FORWARD_ONLY; rollback cannot obscure already-persisted legal evidence.
artifacts.state_or_outcome: DeploymentRelease is the authoritative rollout, rollback, and fail-forward governance object.
artifacts.trigger_or_entry_condition: A candidate is deployed, rolled back, pinned, failed forward, or superseded in one environment.

explicit_gaps.description: ManifestLineageTrace is normatively required by prose but no dedicated schema artifact exists in the current corpus.
Legal start-claim outcomes are prose-defined but are not mirrored as an enum on the manifest_start_claim schema.
Branch reason codes are contractually required in prose but no dedicated schema-backed enum was found for manifest_branch_decision.
The corpus defines the unattended policy value domain and required stage families, but does not publish one canonical tenant-default stage/value assignment matrix.
pc_0015 references ../shared_operating_contract_0014_to_0021.md, but no such file exists under PROMPT/ at generation time.
